name: Fig Loans
description_short: Fig Loans is a Sugar Land, TX-based mission-driven lender offering small installment loans ($100-$1,000) in 6 states. Founded 2015 with United Way. APR 199-211% for installment loans, 4%+ for credit builder loans. 4.9 Trustpilot from 8,600+ reviews.
description_long:
Fig Loans (Fig Tech Inc.) is a mission-driven consumer lender headquartered in Sugar Land, Texas, founded in 2015 in partnership with United Way THRIVE as a safer alternative to payday loans. The company offers small installment loans ranging from $100 to $1,000 in six states: Texas, Missouri, Ohio, Utah, Florida, and California. With a 4.9-star Trustpilot rating from over 8,600 reviews, Fig Loans has built strong consumer trust among its target borrowers.

Fig Loans offers two primary products: installment loans with APRs of 199-211% (still high but with transparent pricing and structured repayment), and credit builder loans with APRs as low as 4% where borrowers make 12 fixed monthly payments and receive their principal back at the end, minus interest — building credit history in the process. The credit builder product is a genuine differentiator that most payday alternative lenders do not offer.

While APRs of 199-211% on installment loans are high, they are structured differently from payday loans: borrowers repay over months in fixed installments rather than a single lump sum on payday. The United Way partnership and social enterprise mission distinguish Fig from for-profit payday lenders. The BBB file was opened in July 2025, so limited BBB data is available.

As a consumer lender, this company competes with both traditional banks and newer fintech personal loan lenders. Borrowers seeking personal loans for bad credit may find more flexible terms through online lenders, while those focused on simplifying payments may benefit from debt consolidation loans with fixed rates. For credit building, secured credit cards and credit builder loans offer structured paths to improvement. Credit monitoring services provide ongoing visibility into credit health, and credit counseling through nonprofit agencies can help consumers create sustainable budgeting plans.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

tiers.name: Personal Installment Loans
features: Build credit with on-time payments reported to bureaus

certifications: Founded in partnership with United Way THRIVE
Loans in 6 states: TX, MO, OH, UT, FL, CA
Also offers credit builder loans (APR as low as 4%)

pros: Fixed monthly payments over 4, 6, or 8-month terms prevent debt-trap rollover cycles common in payday lending
Reports all payments to credit bureaus since 2016, enabling genuine credit-building for borrowers establishing history
Transparent APR disclosure with no hidden fees — stated rate is the actual cost
Available in 40+ states with multiple state options, providing broader geographic access than some lenders
B Corp certified, indicating third-party verification of social responsibility and accountability standards
Comparison tool on website demonstrates concrete savings vs. traditional payday loans with rollover cycles
Installment structure modeled after home loan repayment, creating predictable payoff timeline
cons: APRs around 190% remain very high compared to credit union loans, personal loans, or credit cards — 5-10x higher than mainstream alternatives
Minimum loan amount appears to be $300, which may not serve consumers needing smaller emergency amounts under $200
Website comparison uses worst-case payday scenario (7 rollovers) rather than standard payday loan, potentially overstating relative advantage
No disclosed information about approval rates, credit requirements, or actual APR ranges customers receive — site shows only examples
Limited transparency on alternative loan amounts and terms beyond the $300/4-month example provided
best_for: Consumers who need small emergency loans ($100-$1,000) as a safer payday loan alternative
Individuals in TX, MO, OH, UT, FL, or CA seeking mission-driven lending with transparent terms
People looking for credit builder loans with APRs as low as 4% to establish payment history
Borrowers who value social enterprise lending through United Way partnerships
diagnosis: Fig Loans is one of the more ethical options in the small-dollar lending space, with its United Way partnership and mission-driven approach. The credit builder product (4% APR) is genuinely useful for building credit. Installment loan APRs (199-211%) are high but transparent and better structured than payday loans. The 4.9 Trustpilot from 8,600+ reviews is strong. Best for consumers in the 6 covered states who need $100-$1,000 quickly and want a payday alternative from a socially responsible lender. The credit builder product is worth considering on its own merits.
services: Installment loans with 4, 6, or 8-month repayment terms
Fixed monthly payment structure with predictable payoff dates
Loan amounts starting at $300 with options for larger loans
Credit bureau reporting to all three major bureaus for credit-building
Transparent APR disclosure with no hidden fees
State-specific loan availability (customizable by state selection)
Comparison tools showing savings vs. traditional payday loans
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
